rulings.Case Title: MARGARET D. FOSTER et al VS. JOHN F. BRITTON et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2013, line 1 - CROSS DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustain without leave amend. Cross-complainant fails to comply with a prior order to allege facts showing Regulation 6.15c is not reasonably necessary to effectuate purpose of rent ordinance and is arbitrary, capricious or without a reasonable or rational basis. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JULIUS BRIGGS VS. MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2013, line 2, DEFENDANTS MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION, ALTAMONT HOTEL ASSOCIATES, LP, ALTAMONT HOTEL, INC., AND CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION'S Motion For Protective Order And Request For Sanctions. Continued to January 17, 2013 to be heard with companion motion. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND PARKER-GAYLORD VS. JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2013, line 3. DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY, JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A, AND DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST CONPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. First cause of action - sustain with leave to allege owner occupied; 2nd cause of action - Sustain with leave to amend to allege tender and address Gomes, Frazier and Fontenot re prejudice and defendants standing to foreclose; 3rd cause of action - sustain with leave to allege facts showing loan not assigned to Deutsche Bank; and Chase Bank not authorized to accept payment under Note and Deed of Trust as servicer of loan; 4th cause of action - Sustain without leave to amend, no substantive opposition see section 7 of opposition; 5th cause of action sustain without leave to amend see Ortiz v. Accredited Home Lender 639 F Supp 2d 1159, 1168; 6th cause of action - sustain with leave to reallege as remedy and allege non duplicative basis for declaratory relief. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM YOUNG VS. ERIK FERGUSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Against Deft For Disobedience Of The Court'S Order Dated Dec 28, 2012 And To Compel Deft To Appear For Oral Deposition And Produce Documents
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2013, line 4. PLAINTIFF WILLIAM YOUNG's Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant For Disobedience Of The Court'S Order Dated Dec 28, 2012 And To Compel Defendant To Appear For Oral Deposition And Produce Documents. Written discovery - 1) Grant, subject to opposition plaintiff's request for evidence sanctions per CCP 2033.030(c). Defendant prohibited from producing evidence at trial to support defenses. Deposition - 2) Grant, subject to opposition plaintiff's motion to compel deposition. Defendant shall appear on or before January 18, 2013. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICAELA M LEZCANO VS. GINA MARIE ARRIOLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery From Deft And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2013, line 5. PLAINTIFF MICAELA LEZCANO's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery From Defendant And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Grant, subject to opposition plus sanctions in amount of $960. = (501/REQ)
